bhūmirāmaṇeyyakaṁ.meaning: delightful places; the loveliness of the land

bhūtapubbaṁ.gram: adverb / adjective, compound (bhūta + pubba, “having been before”)
breakdown: Been, happened; past participle of bhavati.

boddhabbaṁ.meaning: should be understood; you should understand
breakdown: Root “to awaken, understand” (budh > boddh- before the suffix).

bojjhaṅga.meaning: factor of enlightenment; constituent of awakening
bojjhaṅga.summary: This compound (bodhi + aṅga) names the seven factors of enlightenment.
breakdown: Enlightenment, awakening (bodhi + aṅga coalesces to bojjhaṅga).
bojjhaṅga.goenka: The bojjhaṅgā (also sambojjhaṅgā) are the seven factors of enlightenment: sati (awareness), dhammavicaya (investigation of the Dhamma), viriya (effort), pīti (rapture), passaddhi (tranquillity), samādhi (concentration) and upekkhā (equanimity). Their perfection is laid out in the Ānāpānassati Sutta, where the meditator develops them one by one to their fulfilment on the path to liberation.

brahmacariyavāso.meaning: living the holy life; the dwelling in brahmacariya
brahmacariyavāso.gram: masculine noun, nominative singular, compound (brahmacariya + vāsa)
breakdown: The holy life; the pure life of practice.

brahmacariyaṁ.meaning: the holy life; faring the path of a brahma; the life of purity
breakdown: Faring, conduct, way of life; from carati “to move, conduct oneself”.

brahmajālasuttavaṇṇanā.meaning: the commentary on the Brahmajāla Sutta (“The Net of Brahmā”; brahma + jāla “net, entanglement”)
brahmajālasuttavaṇṇanā.gram: proper noun (commentary title; vaṇṇanā: explanation, commentarial exposition)

brahmalokaparāyaṇataṁ.meaning: having the Brahmā world as one’s destination; finding one’s support in the Brahmā sphere
breakdown: Destination, final support; attached to, finding one’s support in.

brahmayānarūpaṁ.meaning: having the form of the divine vehicle; the brahma-like vehicle

bravuṁ.gram: verb, aorist 3rd person plural of brūti (√brū)

brūhi.gram: verb, imperative 2nd person singular of brūti (√brū)

brūmi.gram: verb, present tense, 1st person singular of brūti (√brū)

brūsi.gram: verb, present tense, 2nd person singular of brūti (√brū)

buddha,.gram: masculine noun (past participle of bujjhati, √budh “to awaken”)
buddha,.note: uncertain: this headword is a glossary-row artifact (a footnote fragment mentioning the Buddha, the Theravādins and the doctrine of logic); see Buddha.

buddhavaggo, dhammapadapāḷi, khuddakanik.meaning: the Buddha chapter of the Dhammapada, in the Khuddaka Nikāya

buddhānamuppādo.meaning: the arising of Buddhas; the appearance of the Awakened Ones
breakdown: Of the Buddhas; genitive plural (the m bridges in sandhi).

buddhānussatikathā, chaanussatiniddeso,.meaning: the “Discussion of the Recollection of the Buddha” in the exposition of the six recollections of the Visuddhimagga

buddhānussatikathā, dhammānussatikathā,.meaning: the discussions of the recollection of the Buddha, the recollection of the Dhamma and the recollection of the Saṅgha

byantīkaroti.meaning: to abolish; to get rid of, make an end of
byantīkaroti.gram: verb, present tense, 3rd person singular, compound verb (byanta + karoti)
breakdown: To an end (vi + anta “end”, in compound form byantī-).

byappanā/ vyappanā.meaning: application of the mind; fixing of the mind, focusing
breakdown: Fixing, application (of the mind onto its object).

byasanena.gram: neuter noun, instrumental singular of byasana

byattena.meaning: by one experienced; by a learned, competent person
byattena.gram: past participle of byañjayati, instrumental singular (vi + √añj + ta)

byañjanato.meaning: as to the letter; by the word, the characteristic, the distinctive mark
byañjanato.gram: neuter noun, ablative singular of byañjana (vi + √añj + ana)

byādhidhammomhi.meaning: I am of the nature to fall sick; sickness is my nature
breakdown: I am (1st person singular of atthi; dhammo + amhi fuses to dhammomhi).

byāpajjati.meaning: to be malevolent; to have ill will
byāpajjati.gram: verb, present tense, 3rd person singular (vi + ā + √pad)

byāpādo.summary: Byāpāda is one of the five hindrances and one of the unwholesome roots of action.
breakdown: Verbal prefixes (vy- > by- before the vowel).
Root “to go, fall”; byāpāda is the mind “gone wrong” — ill will.
byāpādo.goenka: Byāpādo is ill will — aversion, hatred — one of the principal impurities of the mind. It appears among the five hindrances that block concentration, and as the root that right thought abandons: the thought free from ill will (abyāpādasaṅkappa) is an aspect of sammāsaṅkappa on the Noble Eightfold Path.

byārosanā.meaning: out of anger; injuring in anger
byārosanā.gram: feminine abstract noun (from byāroseti, vi + ā + √rus)

byārosanā out of.note: uncertain: this headword is a glossary-row artifact; the entry is byārosanā with the gloss “out of anger”. See byārosanā.

bādhenti.gram: verb, present tense, 3rd person plural, causative of bādhati (√bādh)

bāhitvā.gram: absolutive (gerund) of bāheti “to ward off, keep away”

bāhusaccañca.gram: neuter abstract noun + particle (bāhusacca + ca, with sandhi ñc)
breakdown: Great learning; the state of having heard much (from bahussuta).
And (saccaṁ + ca fuses to saccañca in sandhi).

bāhābalaparicitā.meaning: accumulated by the strength of one’s arms; earned by one’s own labour
breakdown: Accumulated, gathered; past participle of pacināti/paricināti.

bālā.gram: adjective/masculine noun, nominative plural of bāla

bālāpadānāni.meaning: the characteristics of a fool; the deeds that mark a fool
breakdown: Fool, foolish (bāla + apadāna coalesces to bālāpadāna).

bījagāmabhūtagāmasamārambhā.meaning: injury to seeds and plants; damaging seed-life and plant-life
bījagāmabhūtagāmasamārambhā.gram: masculine noun, ablative singular, compound (bījagāma + bhūtagāma + samārambha)
breakdown: The collection of grown plants; plant-life.
Injury to, destruction of; from saṁ + ā + √rabh.

bījarukkhādikānaṁva.meaning: like seeds, trees and so on
bījarukkhādikānaṁva.gram: compound, genitive plural + particle (bīja + rukkha + ādika + va)
breakdown: Beginning with, and so on; genitive plural.

bījīyati.gram: verb, present tense, 3rd person singular, passive of vījati “to fan”

cakkavattisutta.meaning: the Cakkavatti Sutta, the discourse on the duties of a righteous wheel-turning king
cakkavattisutta.goenka: The Cakkavattisutta describes the different lengths of the human lifespan and their close dependence on the quality of moral standards that are upheld — on social and ethical principles such as sammāvācā, sammākammanto and sammā-ājīvo (right speech, right action and right livelihood). According to this text the lifespan varies from a maximum of 80,000 years down to 10 years as morality declines.

cakkavattivatta’nti.meaning: “the duty of a wheel-turning monarch”, they say
breakdown: Wheel-turning monarch (cakka “wheel” + vattin “one who sets rolling”).
Quotation marker ti fused with the preceding niggahīta (ṁ + ti > nti).

cakkavattī.meaning: a wheel-turning monarch; a universal king who sets the wheel (of righteousness) rolling
cakkavattī.gram: masculine agent noun, nominative singular, compound (cakka + vattin)
breakdown: One who sets rolling, who keeps in motion; from √vatt.

cakkavāḷesu.gram: masculine noun, locative plural of cakkavāḷa
breakdown: Enclosure, rim; cakkavāḷa is a world system, locative plural “in”.
Like, as (iva, shortened and fused in sandhi).
